response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but their profile does not match the core requirements of this role. Their C++ exposure is limited to a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. They lack SQL Server/Oracle experience (MongoDB/MySQL instead), and their financial-domain exposure (RIDE Capital, ~15 months in investment-adjacent fintech) does not cover Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets systems. The bulk of their career is web/full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang.
strengths: Solid engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, and low-latency systems experience (DLang at Dunnhumby)
Some professional C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and systems-level programming background
Brief fintech domain exposure at RIDE Capital plus strong communication skills (workshops, talks, teaching)
concerns: Nowhere near the mandatory 5–10 years of professional C++ on Windows; C++ work is dated (2014–2015) and brief
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets systems experience; RIDE Capital exposure is shallow and not C++-based

key_factors.factor: Missing Fixed Income / capital-markets domain expertise
Strong general engineering craftsmanship and CI/CD skills
